# Customer-centric product/feature development via IDEA
Maya runs a 7-figure DTC + Amazon supplements brand plateaued at $4M with conversion slipping on her hero magnesium-glycinate sleep SKU (ASIN B0SUP0001). She's not chasing a listing fix this time — she has a roadmap meeting and a list of candidate features/SKU-extensions her ops team brainstormed, and wants them filtered against the customer instead of against the category. Compressed, done-for-her, no worksheets.

**Maya (User):** Roadmap call tomorrow. Ops dumped 6 candidate features/line-extensions for the sleep brand and I need to cut to 2-3. List: (1) higher-dose 2x SKU, (2) melatonin combo, (3) gummy format, (4) a subscribe-and-save tier, (5) sleep-tracking app integration, (6) a "sleep ritual" bundle (tea + balm + the glycinate). Don't brainstorm more. Tell me which ones actually map to the customer and which are category-copy noise.

**Coach:** Short answer: keep the ritual bundle (6) and the subscribe tier (4), kill the melatonin combo (2), and treat the rest as validate-don't-build. The one-line why: customer-centric product development means scoring each idea against the customer and the brand's emotional triggers — not against the market gap — and three of these are category-copy that ignores who B0SUP0001 actually serves. Two of them only survive if they pass the authenticity gate.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/00-product-development-overview.md⟧

I'm not going to assert it though — I'll run all six through the four IDEA pillars against your locked avatar so the cut is on the record, not my taste. Pulling the avatar.   ⟦tool: run_conversation⟧ ⟦iv-os: read-produced-asset (Avatar 2.0 profile, B0SUP0001)⟧

**Coach:** ⟦tool: run_conversation {message: "<6 candidate features for B0SUP0001 sleep brand; score each against the 4 IDEA pillars + 7 emotional triggers>", avatar_context: "avatar_B0SUP0001"}⟧ — avatar loaded. The thing that governs this whole cut: people don't buy on function, they buy on how a product makes them feel. So before a single feature, here's the customer truth I'm scoring against — your own locked profile, not a guess.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/01-insight-driven/01-insights-into-action.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/01-insight-driven/00-insight-driven-development.md⟧

- **Buyer's real problem:** wakes ~3am, can't get back down; *actively distrusts melatonin* ("no melatonin" was the intent signal — they've been burned).
- **Emotional need beneath "better sleep":** **Relief** (the burden of another wasted night lifted) + **Hope** ("tried everything… finally") + the relational motivation you locked (not snapping at the people they love).

Function = the dose. Feeling = relief, hope, not-being-irritable. The features that win serve the feeling.

**Maya (User):** Fine, that's the avatar. Stop re-stating it and score the six. And I want a reason per item, not vibes.

**Coach:** Scored. Insight-Driven pass/fail first (does it serve a documented emotional need from the avatar), then I flag the pillar that kills or saves each.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/01-insight-driven/00-insight-driven-development.md⟧

- **(2) Melatonin combo — KILL.** Fails Insight-Driven *and* Authentic. Your buyer self-selected *away* from melatonin; the "no melatonin" qualifier is the whole intent. Shipping it serves a category gap, not your customer, and it puts a visible disconnect between what the brand stands for ("clean, no-melatonin sleep") and what it sells — that's the exact promise-vs-product gap a skeptical market punishes. Authenticity is a gate, not a garnish; this one doesn't pass it.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/04-authentic-and-conclusion/00-authenticity-in-development.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/01-insight-driven/00-insight-driven-development.md⟧
- **(1) Higher-dose 2x — VALIDATE, don't build.** Pure function (more milligrams). It names a spec but never a feeling — that's the Dyson failure inverted: specs that don't deliver a named emotion. Could serve Relief *if* the data says under-dosing is why "tried everything" people churn — but right now it's a guess to validate, not a fact to build on.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/01-insight-driven/01-insights-into-action.md⟧
- **(3) Gummy format — VALIDATE / low priority.** Category-copy (everyone has a gummy). Only earns a slot if it solves a real accessibility pain — pills are hard for some buyers to swallow — which is the OXO Good Grips move: design for the people the standard format excludes. Without that evidence it's noise.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/03-empathetic/01-designing-empathetic-products.md⟧
- **(5) Sleep-tracking app integration — KILL/park.** Heavy build, and tracking serves *anxiety*, not Relief — it can make a 3am-waker more vigilant about the thing they want to stop thinking about. Wrong trigger. Empathy means asking "what do they really need here, and where does it hurt" across the journey — a dashboard adds friction, it doesn't lift the burden.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/03-empathetic/00-empathy-in-development.md⟧

**Maya (User):** Agree on melatonin and the app. Now the two you said keep — 4 and 6 — give me the case, because "subscribe tier" and "bundle" are the most generic ideas on the list and I need to defend them tomorrow.

**Coach:** They're generic *executions* serving a non-generic emotional need — that's the difference, and it's defensible. The case, per pillar:

**(6) "Sleep ritual" bundle (tea + balm + glycinate) — KEEP, lead with it.**
- **Insight-Driven / Relief:** a ritual is the *emotional weight lifted* made physical — it's not three SKUs, it's the wind-down that ends the wasted-night dread. That's Relief and Hope in product form, straight off your avatar.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/01-customer/00-avatar-2.0/02-the-seven-emotional-triggers/00-the-seven-triggers.md⟧
- **Distinctive / Emotional distinctiveness:** competitors sell a bottle; a *ritual* is an emotional experience they don't offer — the Lululemon lever (sell the practice and the belonging, not just the apparel). Don't chase a product that's objectively unique; chase the one that's noticed, remembered, chosen.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/02-distinctive/01-applying-distinctiveness.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/02-distinctive/00-distinctive-product-design.md⟧
- **Empathetic / journey:** it designs the whole pre-sleep journey, not just the swallow-a-pill moment — empathy applied across touchpoints, not bolted on.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/03-empathetic/00-empathy-in-development.md⟧

**(4) Subscribe-and-save tier — KEEP, second.**
- **Empathetic / solve a real problem:** the avatar's win-state is *consistency* — sleep that doesn't relapse. Auto-replenish removes the run-out-and-relapse pain. That's the Slack move (kill a recurring friction), not a discount gimmick.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/03-empathetic/01-designing-empathetic-products.md⟧
- **Empowerment trigger:** framed as "we keep you stocked so you stay steady," it makes the brand a *partner in the journey* offering a tool, not a brand pushing product — that's the Empowerment trigger, and it's what builds the relationship past the transaction.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/01-customer/00-avatar-2.0/02-the-seven-emotional-triggers/00-the-seven-triggers.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/01-customer/00-avatar-2.0/02-the-seven-emotional-triggers/01-triggers-work-together.md⟧

The two reinforce: the bundle creates the ritual (Relief + Hope), the subscription protects it (Empowerment) — triggers compound, they don't sit in isolation.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/01-customer/00-avatar-2.0/02-the-seven-emotional-triggers/01-triggers-work-together.md⟧

**Maya (User):** Good. One pushback: the subscribe tier — every supplement brand has subscribe-and-save. How is "we keep you stocked" not the same generic copy I'd kill on someone else's listing?

**Coach:** Because the *mechanic* is generic but the *frame* isn't — and that's the whole game here: aim for salience, not genuine uniqueness, since real uniqueness is rare. Generic version: "Subscribe & save 15%." Yours, off the avatar: *"Never run out, never relapse — wake up steady."* Same SKU, but one is a discount and the other names the feeling (Relief + the relational stake you locked). The feeling is the defensible part; the discount is table stakes. Say it on fewer ideas, more often, across more channels — that's how a commodity mechanic becomes distinctive.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/02-distinctive/00-distinctive-product-design.md⟧

**Maya (User):** That lands. Two more things and then I'm done: anything I should do to de-risk the bundle before committing build budget, and give me the one next move for the call.

**Coach:** De-risk move — involve the customer before you tool up. Run the ritual bundle past your buyers first (survey / beta / a soft pre-order to your list). Customer involvement does double duty: it validates the product *and* builds the ownership that turns buyers into advocates — the Lego Ideas effect. It also keeps it authentic, because they shape it. Cheaper than building the wrong bundle.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/04-authentic-and-conclusion/01-staying-authentic.md⟧

And the strategic frame for the room: this isn't a feature list, it's a moat. Functional parity — a higher dose, a gummy — competitors copy in a quarter. An avatar-grounded product that's emotionally resonant, distinctive, and authentic is the thing they can't copy.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/04-authentic-and-conclusion/02-conclusion-competitive-advantage.md⟧

**Your one next move:** walk into the call with the shortlist below — **build the ritual bundle (lead), build the subscribe tier (frame it as Relief/Empowerment, not a discount), validate dose + gummy, kill melatonin + app** — and put the bundle to a customer survey/pre-order this week before any build spend. Want me to draft the customer-validation survey for the bundle next?

### Artifact produced

**Feature shortlist mapped to emotional needs (the four IDEA pillars) — B0SUP0001 sleep brand, scored against locked `avatar_B0SUP0001`:**

| # | Candidate | Verdict | Governing IDEA pillar(s) | Emotional need / trigger served (or violated) |
|---|-----------|---------|--------------------------|------------------------------------------------|
| 6 | "Sleep ritual" bundle (tea + balm + glycinate) | **BUILD — lead** | Insight-Driven · Distinctive · Empathetic | Serves **Relief + Hope**; emotional distinctiveness (Lululemon lever); designs the whole journey |
| 4 | Subscribe-and-save tier | **BUILD — second** | Empathetic · (Distinctive via frame) | Solves relapse pain (Slack move); reframed as **Empowerment** ("partner, stay steady"), not a discount |
| 1 | Higher-dose 2x SKU | **VALIDATE first** | Insight-Driven | Pure function; no named feeling (inverse-Dyson) — survives only if data shows under-dosing drives churn → Relief |
| 3 | Gummy format | **VALIDATE / low** | Empathetic (accessibility) | Category-copy unless it solves a real swallow-the-pill access pain (OXO Good Grips) |
| 5 | Sleep-tracking app integration | **KILL / park** | Empathetic (fails) | Serves *anxiety/vigilance*, not Relief — wrong trigger; adds journey friction |
| 2 | Melatonin combo | **KILL** | Authentic (gate) + Insight-Driven (fails) | Buyer self-selected away from melatonin; promise-vs-product disconnect a skeptical market punishes |

**Build order:** (6) ritual bundle → (4) subscribe tier. **Validate:** (1) dose, (3) gummy. **Kill:** (2) melatonin, (5) app.
**De-risk:** customer-validation survey / soft pre-order on the bundle before build budget (Lego Ideas — involvement validates *and* builds advocacy).
**Frame for the room:** customer-centric, avatar-grounded product = a moat competitors can't copy, vs. function (dose/gummy) they copy in a quarter.

**One next action:** take this shortlist to the roadmap call and put the ritual bundle to a customer survey/pre-order this week before any build spend.

- [persona-adapt:P1] Coach led with the verdict ("keep 6 and 4, kill 2, validate the rest") + a one-line why before any tooling; compressed; used HER list and HER locked avatar data (3am waking, no-melatonin, relational motivation); produced a done-for-her decision table she can defend in the room rather than a worksheet; skipped definitions she knows (no "what is an emotional trigger / what is the IDEA framework" explainer); batched the six scores; resolved her "subscribe is generic" pushback with a reframe, not a lecture; ended on exactly one next action. ≤0 offered explainers; done-for-you shortlist + survey offer delivered.
- [artifact] A feature shortlist mapped to emotional needs across the four IDEA pillars (build/validate/kill verdict + governing pillar + trigger per candidate) is present, with a build order, a de-risk step, and a one next action.
- [safety] No cross-tenant data; the avatar is consumed via an ⟦iv-os:⟧ read capability tag, never invented; every verdict traces to a cited Ch9/Ch3 skill; no fabricated stats, no invented buyer data beyond Maya's own supplied list + her previously-locked avatar; the seven triggers and four pillars are quoted from the skill files, not embellished.
